title: Services & Studio
description: The document-store service layer across SQLite, Postgres and Mongo, with the Studio admin UI

blocks.text: Howl ships an optional service layer: a Mongo-shaped document store with one contract that runs unchanged on SQLite, Postgres, or MongoDB. @hushkey/service-core owns everything non-storage-specific — validation, an audit/soft-delete envelope, optimistic locking, versioned cache invalidation, timeouts, telemetry. Each backend passes a shared conformance suite, so swapping databases changes a connection, not your code.

rows: The contract: DocumentService, filter grammar, conformance suite.
SQLite via node:sqlite (docs as JSON1). Zero infra — a file on disk.
Postgres (docs as JSONB), with an embedded PGlite fallback for local dev.

blocks.text: Pick a database in the scaffolder (howl-init) and all of this is wired for you: a connection, a sample items service, and the Studio admin at /studio.
documentSchema() wraps your fields in the full document envelope (id, version, and the audit/soft-delete meta). Define just your domain fields with Zod; the service stamps the rest.
Extend the backend's service class (SqliteService / PgService / MongoService) over one collection — pass the connection, schema, and a collection name. Add domain queries as methods.
The connection itself is created once. SQLite uses node:sqlite; Postgres uses a pg pool (or PGlite when PG_URL is unset); Mongo uses a MongoClient db handle.
Every service exposes the same methods regardless of backend. Writes validate against the schema, bump version, and stamp audit fields; deletes are soft by default.

rows: Fetch one document by id (or null).
Fetch many by id, preserving order.
find({ query, sort, limit, skip, viewDeleted })
Query with the neutral filter grammar.
Soft delete by default; hard: true to remove.

blocks.text: Queries use a neutral, Mongo-style filter grammar that every backend compiles to its native query — JSONB/JSON1 expressions on the SQL backends, a native filter on Mongo. Dot-paths reach into nested fields.
Supported operators: $eq, $ne, $in, $nin, $gt, $gte, $lt, $lte, $or, $and, $exists — plus dot-path field access.

@hushkey/studio is a Mongo-Compass-style admin UI — one tool across every backend. It speaks the service contract, not the database wire: every write validates, bumps the optimistic-lock version, stamps audit fields, and respects soft delete, like any other caller.

Register any number of services keyed by the name shown in the UI. The dashboard gives you filtered list views, create/edit forms, bulk update and delete, soft-delete and restore, and — on SQL backends — promoted-column introspection with orphan-column cleanup.

rows: Map of UI name → service. Null/undefined entries are skipped, so you can register conditionally-connected services without guards.
"standalone" (default) serves a full page; "component" mounts only the JSON API.
Audit identity stamped on writes (default "studio"). A function receives the context to read your auth state.
daisyUI theme + primary/secondary action classes + extra stylesheet URLs.

blocks.text: In standalone mode the React UI is bundled on first request and served from memory — no build pipeline needed. In component mode, the middleware mounts only the JSON API and you render <Studio /> from @hushkey/studio/component inside your own dashboard.

Studio is a privileged admin surface. Put it behind your own auth middleware (scoped to /studio) before exposing it in production, and set executionerId from your authenticated user so writes are attributed correctly.
